report_name: Cyberattack Disrupts Emergency Communications in Massachusetts Towns

attack_patterns.name: Endpoint Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: The cyberattack caused a widespread outage of public safety computer systems, including computer-aided dispatch (CAD) and records management systems (RMS), and took non-emergency phone lines offline, degrading emergency response capabilities.

attack_patterns.name: Denial of Control
attack_patterns.description: The attack disrupted computer-aided dispatch (CAD) and records management systems (RMS), preventing operators from interacting with normal process controls and forcing them to work manually.

attack_patterns.name: Denial of View
attack_patterns.description: The outage of computer-aided dispatch (CAD) and records management systems (RMS) prevented dispatchers from having a normal view of emergency response resources and incident status.

campaigns.name: Cyberattack on Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center
campaigns.description: A cyberattack beginning on April 2, 2026, that disrupted the computer systems and non-emergency phone lines of the Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center in Massachusetts, impacting public safety operations for several towns.

identities.name: Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center
identities.description: A regional 911 dispatch hub in northern Massachusetts targeted by a cyberattack, causing significant disruption to its computer systems and non-emergency phone lines.

identities.name: Town of Pepperell
identities.description: A town in northern Massachusetts whose emergency services are provided by the Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center, which was impacted by a cyberattack.

identities.name: Town of Ashby
identities.description: A town in northern Massachusetts whose emergency services are provided by the Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center, which was impacted by a cyberattack.

identities.name: Town of Groton
identities.description: A town in northern Massachusetts whose emergency services are provided by the Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center, which was impacted by a cyberattack.

indicators.name: Sudden Cessation of CAD System Logs
indicators.pattern:
title: Sudden Cessation of Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) System Logs
id: 9b8a0c1d-e2f3-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects a sudden stop in logging from Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) systems, which could indicate a system outage caused by a cyberattack such as ransomware or denial of service. This rule should be configured in a SIEM to alert when a critical log source is silent for an unusual period.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/massachusetts-emergency-communications-system-impacted-by-cyberattack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1499
  - attack.t0813
  - attack.t0815
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  product: siem
  service: alert
detection:
  selection:
    RuleName: 'Health - Log Source Stopped Reporting'
    Source: 'Computer-Aided Dispatch System'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate system maintenance or network outage.
  - Misconfiguration of log shipping or SIEM health monitoring.
indicators.description: Detects a sudden stop in logging from Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) systems, which could indicate a system outage caused by a cyberattack as described in the report.

indicators.name: Loss of Dispatch Console Network Heartbeat
indicators.pattern:
title: Loss of Dispatch Console Network Heartbeat
id: 1d2e3f4a-5b6c-7d8e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects the absence of expected periodic network traffic (heartbeats) from critical 911 dispatch consoles. A simultaneous loss of heartbeats from multiple consoles is a strong indicator of a widespread system or network outage, potentially due to a cyberattack.
references:
  - https://therecord.media/massachusetts-emergency-communications-system-impacted-by-cyberattack
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-04
tags:
  - attack.t1499
  - attack.t0813
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: zeek
detection:
  # This is a conceptual rule. A real implementation would require a SIEM to baseline normal traffic and alert on deviation.
  # The logic is to alert if no traffic is seen from a list of critical assets for a given time window.
  selection:
    source_ip:
      - '10.9.1.10' # Placeholder for dispatch console IP range
      - '10.9.1.11'
    destination_ip:
      - '10.9.1.1' # Placeholder for CAD server IP
  timeframe: 5m
  condition: not selection
falsepositives:
  - Network device failure.
  - Scheduled maintenance of dispatch systems.
indicators.description: Detects the absence of expected periodic network traffic (heartbeats) from critical 911 dispatch consoles, indicating a potential system outage.
